title: Battle for Outbound Flight
content:
{{Top|leg|canon=Confrontation with the Vagaari|title=Battle for ''Outbound Flight''}} {{Battle |prev=[[Destruction of Special Task Force One]][[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] takes place [[Standard year/Legends|five years]] after the [[Battle of Naboo/Legends|Battle of Naboo]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], therefore, the events of ''Outbound Flight'' must take place in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]]. As the events of the novel take place in the same time period, the Destruction of Special Task Force One takes place before the Battle of Outbound Flight |conc= |next= |image=[[File:OutboundFlight-cover.jpg]] |name=Battle for ''Outbound Flight'' |conflict=[[Outbound Flight Project]][[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] |date=[[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]] |place=A [[Unidentified Unknown Regions star system (red sun)|star system]], [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] |result=Severe damage to ''[[Outbound Flight]]'' and Vagaari fleet |side1=''Outbound Flight''† |side2=[[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]] |side3=[[Vagaari Empire]] |side4= |commanders1=[[Jorus C'baoth]]† |commanders2=[[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo]] |commanders3=A [[Unidentified Miskara (Jorj Car'das)|Miskara]]† |commanders4= |unit1= |unit2=*[[Chiss Expansionary Fleet/Legends|Chiss Expansionary Fleet]] **[[Picket Force Two/Legends|Picket Force Two]] |unit3= |unit4= |forces1=6 [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught''-class heavy cruisers]] |forces2=*''[[Springhawk/Legends|Springhawk]]'' *[[Whirlwind (Chiss)/Legends|''Whirlwind'']] *9 Chiss [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] *About 1,000 [[Vulture-class starfighter/Legends|droid starfighters]] *10 [[radiation bomb]]s *2 [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]] *4 [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-Series battle droids]] |forces3=*Over 200 Vagaari vessels **Over 100 Vagaari warships |forces4= |casual1=*Severe damage to Outbound Flight and deaths of all but 58 passengers *Jorus C'baoth |casual2=*Most droid starfighters |casual3=Most of the Vagaari fleet *Miskara and crew |casual4= |civilian= }} {{Quote|There wasn't anything more to be heard. It was intercepted by a task force outside Old Republic space and destroyed.''"
"''How do you know?''"
"''Because I was the force's commander.|[[Grand Admiral/Legends|Grand Admiral]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Thrawn]] and [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Gilad Pellaeon/Legends|Gilad Pellaeon]] discussing the battle, years later|''[[Heir to the Empire]]''}} The '''battle for ''Outbound Flight''''' was a three way battle between ''[[Outbound Flight]]'', [[Picket Force Two/Legends|Picket Force Two]], and the [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]] fleet. It culminated in the deaths of nearly all aboard Outbound Flight and most of the Vagaari fleet. [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]]' original objective was fulfilled with this result. The battle came to be known as the '''Devastation''' by the [[Survivors (Outbound Flight)|survivors]] of the [[Outbound Flight Colony]]. ==Prelude== [[Force Commander]] [[Mitth'raw'nuruodo/Legends|Mitth'raw'nuruodo's]] Picket Force Two waited in an area that was along ''[[Outbound Flight]]''{{'s}} vector, as well as in between the [[Geroon]] [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] and [[Crustai]]. With the [[Whirlwind (Chiss)/Legends|Whirlwind's]] [[Gravity well projector/Legends|gravity well projector]] running, ''Outbound Flight'' was yanked from hyperspace. Thrawn attempted to negotiate with the fallen [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Jorus C'baoth]] to convince him to either return to the [[Galactic Republic]] or continue along a different vector, as their current destination was where the [[Yuuzhan Vong]] were rumored to be. C'baoth refused, so Thrawn was forced to keep ''Outbound Flight'' trapped here. Meanwhile Jorj Car'das went to the Vagaari fleet in order to convince them to attack Crustai. He was successful by giving a gift of [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|battle droids]] and [[Droideka/Legends|droidekas]]. Unknown to both Car'das and the Vagaari, the droids were programmed to kill the Vagaari crew at Thrawn's command. The Vagaari departed with their entire fleet, only to be brought out of hyperspace as well. ==The battle== [[File:Outbound_Flight.jpg|thumb|left|180px|C'baoth Force-chokes Thrawn.]] The Vagaari immediately broadcast hostile messages, which caused the Jedi to paralyze the entire fleet with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Thrawn used this opportunity to launch the droid starfighters against the Vagaari, as well as to activate the "gift" battle droids in order to kill the [[Unidentified Miskara (Jorj Car'das)|Miskara]]. The droid starfighters [[Hull skimming|attacked]] close enough to get in between the living shields of the Vagaari, and destroy the hull itself. The [[Wound in the Force/Legends|deaths]] of thousands of Vagaari caused the Jedi to become weakened. Thrawn then ordered his forces to attack. They hit the weapons blisters and shield generators but left the rest of the vessel alone. Unfortunately, this killed most of the Jedi, as they were manning the weapons at this time. Thrawn again asked ''Outbound Flight'' to return home, but in response, Jorus C'baoth turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and began to [[Telekinesis/Legends|choke]] Thrawn. With the Chiss unable to stop C'baoth's attack, [[Kinman Doriana/Legends|Kinman Doriana]] activated a switch that caused a change in the droid starfighters programming. The droids stopped their attack on the Vagaari and rammed into ''Outbound Flight'', opening it to space. Ten pairs of fighters were equipped with radiation bombs and detonated inside ''Outbound Flight'', killing nearly everybody, including C'baoth. The remaining Vagaari vessels, no longer under attack, fled from the area and into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. The [[Wound in the Force/Legends|deaths]] of thousands of Vagaari caused the Jedi to become weakened. Thrawn then ordered his forces to attack . They hit the weapons blisters and shield generators, but left the rest of the vessel alone. Unfortunately, this killed most of the Jedi, as they were manning the weapons at this time. Thrawn again asked ''Outbound Flight'' to return home, but in response, Jorus C'baoth turned to the [[Dark side of the Force/Legends|dark side]] and began to [[Telekinesis/Legends|choke]] Thrawn. With the Chiss unable to stop C'baoth's attack, [[Kinman Doriana/Legends|Kinman Doriana]] activated a switch that caused a change in the droid starfighters programming. The droids stopped their attack on the Vagaari and rammed into ''Outbound Flight'', opening it to space. Ten pairs of fighters were equipped with radiation bombs and detonated inside ''Outbound Flight'', killing nearly everybody, including C'baoth. The remaining Vagaari vessels, no longer under attack, fled from the area and into [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]]. ==Aftermath== Darth Sidious' original objective to destroy ''Outbound Flight'' succeeded, although in an unexpected way. Thrawn would regret the result, as none of the civilians needed to die, and the Vagaari escaped. Three hours later, a Chiss [[Chaf'orm'bintrano's fleet|fleet]] under the [[Chaf family/Legends|Fifth Ruling Family]] lead by [[Aristocra/Legends|Aristocra]] [[Chaf'orm'bintrano]] arrived to take possession of the Vagaari fleet (and later ''Outbound Flight''). A series of delays and diversions allowed [[Lorana Jinzler]] and [[Mitth'ras'safis/Legends|Mitth'ras'safis]], Thrawn's brother, to escape with ''Outbound Flight'', thus preventing a critical imbalance in Chiss society. On top of losing his brother, many in the [[Ruling Families/Legends|Ruling Families]] still saw Thrawn's actions as tantamount to murder. Although Thrawn was able to convice the Ruling Families to keep his position in the [[Fleet/Legends|fleet]] during''[[Vision of the Future]]'' a meeting held on [[Chiss Ascendancy/Legends|Chiss Ascendancy]]'s [[Capital/Legends|capital]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Csilla/Legends|Csilla]],{{GamerCite|5|The University of Sanbra Guide to Intelligent Life: The Chiss}} from that point on, his actions were watched carefully. ==Appearances== *[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']] {{Ab|Outbound Flight (unabridged audiobook)}} {{1st|in book}} *[[Outbound Flight (abridged
